a: Absolutely! This is a family event designed for all ages. There is a dedicated Children's Zone with Bala Vihar games, crafts, and activities. Children are welcome to participate in the chanting as well — it is a beautiful gift to introduce them to the Hanuman Chalisā.

Registration is free and strongly encouraged so we can plan seating, prasad, and programme booklets. Walk-ins are welcome, but registered devotees will receive updates and event communications. Register at vayuputra.org.

q: Do I need to know Sanskrit or Hindi to participate?
a: Not at all! Programme booklets with transliteration in Roman script and English meanings will be provided. The Chalisā has been transliterated so anyone can chant along regardless of language background.

Bring a yoga mat or small cushion to sit on, a water bottle, sunscreen, and your Hanuman Chalisā booklet (or use ours). Offering items such as flowers, fruits, and coconut are welcome. Traditional attire is encouraged but not required.

Yes! We need 500+ Vayu Sevaks for Hospitality, Food Management, Seva Coordination, Outreach, Stage & Audio-Visual Setup, Children's Zone, Parking, First Aid, and more. Sign up at vayuputra.org. Every act of seva earns Hanumanji's blessings for your family.

Free on-site parking is available at DuPage County Fairgrounds, 2015 Manchester Rd, Wheaton IL 60187. Arrive early as the lot may fill up. By Metra: take the Union Pacific West Line to Wheaton Station, then a short rideshare.

q: What are the Havan seating zones?
a: There are 4 seating zones inside the Yajnashala: Anjaneya, Bajrang, Chiranjeevi, and Dheera. Registered Havan participants check in at Building 2 from 8:30 AM and receive a wristband with their assigned zone.

YogaMaala is a collective Surya Namaskar session from 8:00–9:30 AM, offering 54 rounds (6 sets of 9). All levels are welcome. You may participate in 1 to 6 sets. Please bring a yoga mat, water bottle, and towel. Contact (224) 280-1100 to register.

Community prasad will be distributed after the Havan. A variety of vegetarian and vegan food vendors will be present at the Family Mela throughout the day. All food served is strictly vegetarian in keeping with the sanctity of the Havan.

q: How can I sponsor or donate?
a: Seva and sponsorship tiers range from free (Ramaduta Seva) to $5,001 (Mukhya Yajaman). Visit vayuputra.org/register-sponsors to see all options. All contributions directly support the Havan and Chinmaya Mission programs.
